name: Classic Sour Cream Raisin Pie
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1. Place the unbaked pie shell on a baking sheet.
2. Beat 2 large eggs in a bowl.
3. Whisk together 1 cup sour cream, 3/4 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg until well blended.
4. Stir in 1 cup raisins.
5. Pour the filling into the pie shell.
6. Bake below the oven center in a moderate hot oven (375°F) for 40 minutes, just until set.
7. Cool. If desired, top servings with sour cream.
Pie Shell:
1. In a bowl, combine flour and salt.
2. Cut in shortening until particles are pea size.
3. Sprinkle with milk to make a stiff dough.
4. Roll out on a lightly floured board or cloth to fit an 8-inch pie tin.
5. Fold the edge under and flute the rim.
